fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: California Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity california, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to california, respiratory therapist compact california, california respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: California Respiratory Care Board
board.parent_dept: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)
board.mailing_address:
Respiratory Care Board of California
3750 Kilroy Airport Way, Suite 325
Sacramento, CA 95826

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $300 application fee + $49 out-of-state fingerprint card fee
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Entry Level Examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in California.

steps.title: Create a BreEZe Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the California BreEZe online application system at breeze.ca.gov. This is the first step before you can access any application forms.

steps.title: Submit Online Application via BreEZe
steps.description: Log in to your BreEZe account and complete the 'Application for Licensure' for Respiratory Care Practitioner. Ensure all sections are filled out accurately.

steps.title: Provide Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your CoARC-approved respiratory care program to the Respiratory Care Board. If applying by endorsement with an RRT credential, submit proof of your NBRC RRT credential.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprinting for Background Check
steps.description: Submit to a Live Scan fingerprinting process. Obtain the 'Request for Live Scan Service' form from the Board's website. If residing outside California, you can request fingerprint cards for submission.

steps.title: Pass NBRC Examinations or Provide RRT Proof
steps.description: Ensure the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) directly reports your passing scores for the Therapist Multiple-Choice (TMC) Examination and the Clinical Simulation Examination (CSE) to the California Respiratory Care Board, or provide proof of your current RRT credential if applying by endorsement.

steps.title: Pay Application and Initial License Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable Application Fee through your BreEZe account. Once your application is approved, you will be notified to pay the Initial License Fee before your license is issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a California respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does California have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to California?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

fees_endorsement.jurisprudence_exam_fee: $0 (Law & Ethics course may have separate provider fee)

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in California
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in California, users must create an account and submit their application through the BreEZe online application system.

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to breeze.ca.gov and click 'Register a New Account'. Follow the prompts to create your user profile, which includes providing personal information and setting up security questions, before you can start an application.
